original: Peggy Whitson, American astronaut, 1st woman to command International Space Station, US record for most days in space, born in Mt Ayr, Iowa
United Arab Emirates is the fifth entity to successfully orbit a probe around Mars, as probe Hope begins to study the planet's atmosphere.
Australian National University scientists discover the oldest known star at 13.6 billion years old
Halley's Comet reaches 30th perihelion (closest approach to Sun)
East German speed skater Karin Enke wins the 1,500m in world record 2:03.42 at the Sarajevo Winter Olympics; also wins 1,000m gold
Johann Olav Koss skates world record 5k (6:41:73)
Yodeler Thomas School of Germany achieves 22 tones and 15 falsettos in a single second
The Beatles first gig at Liverpool's Cavern Club; they would play there nearly 300 times over the next two years
Tomb of Tutanchamon's treasurer Maya found in Egypt
Fox cartoon series "The Simpsons" airs 167th episode; longest-running animated series in cartoon history
Apollo 14 returns to Earth

Fastest conventional plane flight from New York to London made by British Airways Boeing 747-436 in 4 hours and 56 minutes; average speed of 1,327 km/h / 825 mph, aided by tailwinds from due to Storm Ciara
1st flight of the Boeing 747 jumbo jet
Slopestyle snowboarding event makes Olympic debut at Sochi Winter Games; American Jamie Anderson is inaugural women's gold medallist
Satchel Paigebecomes 1st Negro League player elected to Baseball Hall of Fame
Spacecraft Galileo flies by Venus at an altitude of 10,000 miles (16,000 km) in a gravity assist maneuver to gain speed on its way to Jupiter
STS-63 - 2nd mission of US/Russian Shuttle-Mir Program goes into space
Irish music and dance show "Riverdance" first opens in Dublin, Ireland with lead dancerMichael Flatley

Super Bowl LIX, Caesars Superdome, New Orleans, Louisiana: Philadelphia Eagles claim second title in franchise history with a 40-22 win over 2-time defending champion Kansas City Chiefs; MVP:Jalen Hurts, PH, QB
Solar Orbiter probe attached to an Atlas rocket lifts off from Cape Canaveral
1st test flight of Boeing 727 jet
Soviet Soyuz 17 returns to Earth
Charles Kingsford-Smith, Australian pioneering aviator (1st transpacific flight), born in Brisbane, Australia (d. 1935)
Frank Frazetta [Frazzetta], American fantasy illustrator of comic book, paperback, album cover art, born in Brooklyn, New York City (d. 2010)
Brian Greene, American physicist known for his work on superstring theory (The Elegant Universe), born in New York City
Ulrich Walter, German physicist and astronaut, flew on Space Shuttle Columbia STS-55, born in Iserlohn, West Germany
Aletta Jacobs, 1st Dutch female physician and women's suffrage activist, born in Sappemeer, Netherlands (d. 1929)
92nd Academy Awards: "Parasite" 1st non English film to win Best Picture, Best Director Bong Joon-ho, Best ActorJoaquin Phoenix, Best ActressRenée Zellweger
Kendrick Lamarperforms halftime at Super Bowl LIX, with an appearance bySamuel L. Jackson
First appearance of the Beatles on "TheEd SullivanShow", live from New York; broadcast draws 73.7 million television viewers
American singer-songwriter and actor Paul Williams sings the torch song "Here's That Rainy Day" in full Planet of the Apes make-up and costume onJohnny Carson's "The Tonight Show"
West German team swims world record 4x200 m freestyle (7:05.17)
Terry Norris knocks downSugar Ray Leonardtwice and beats him to defend his WBC Super Welterweight title
Rick Barryof the Houston Rockets is the 1st in NBA history to score eight 3-pointers in a game
Joe Louisbeats Arturo Godoy in 15 for heavyweight boxing title
MLB St Louis Browns sign pitcherSatchel Paigeat age 45
Kevin Johnson (Phoenix) ends NBA free throw streak of 57 games
Oscar Charleston selected to baseball's Hall of Fame
James King, American basketball player (Olympic gold 1968), born in New Orleans, Louisiana
Lloyd Keasor, American freestyle wrestler (World C'ship gold 68kg, Olympic silver 1976), born in Pumphrey, Maryland
"Stompin' Tom" Connors, Canadian country and folk singer-songwriter ("The Hockey Song"), born in St. John, New Brunswick, (d. 2013)
-51°F (-46°C), Vanderbilt, Michigan (state record)
-63°F (-53°C), Moran, Wyoming (state record)
Massive storm across south-east England hits Cinque Ports realines the coastline, New Romney port now a mile inland
Quake in San Fernando Valley kills 64 & causes over $½B damage
1st Japanese arrive in Hawaii
Snow on Mauna Loa, Hawaii
-5°F (-20.6°C) in Sicily
7th largest snowfall in NYC history (42.4 cm, 16.7")

US PresidentUlysses S Grantsigns law resulting in US Army Signal Service’s establishment of its "Division of Telegrams and Reports for the Benefit of Commerce", later known as the National Weather Service
House of Representatives electsJohn Quincy Adams6th US president
Jamaica signs agreement to become independent
Dutch 2nd Chamber accepts crematory law
Giuseppe Verdi's comic opera "Falstaff" premieres at La Scala Teatro in Milan, Italy
Haifa Technion (Israel) opens
Jacques Monod, French biochemist (1966 Nobel Prize for Physiology or Medicine - genetic control of enzyme and virus synthesis), born in Paris, France (d. 1976)
Norman E. Shumway, American pioneer cardiac transplant surgeon (1st US adult human to human heart transplantation), born in Kalamazoo, Michigan (d. 2006)
Blanche Calloway, American jazz vocalist, bandleader (Twelve Clouds of Joy), civil rights activist, and broadcaster, born in Rochester, New York (d. 1978)
Walter Page, American jazz double bassist, saxophonist, tuba player, and bandleader (Blue Devils; Count Basie Orchestra), born in Gallatin, Missouri (d. 1957)
Ernest Tubb, American country musician ("Walking the Floor Over You"), born in Crisp, Texas (d. 1984)
